entry.title: 每日一曲-Mother
entry.content: Every time I heard this song, I feel depressed. This is not feigned sadness to pretend I am unique. The lyric and melody really go into my heart. When you understand what this song is sing, you will cry. Not so many people understand the feeling of having such a mother. Mother or father, whatever, control everything of you. Your study, your interest, your girl friend, your living and even your mind. Mother will never leave you alone no matter how wishful you are. You have original sin because you were born in this family. I don't know, how long it takes for us to escape from such a mother. I feel scaring because what mother will do if she is going to lose her power. My children will suffer for this and this is inevitable.

entry.title: leetcode(3) dichotomy
entry.content:
69 Sqrt(x) (easy)

Problem description:
**Implement int sqrt(int x).Compute and return the square root of x, where x is guaranteed to be a non-negative integer.Since the return type is an integer, the decimal digits are truncated and only the integer part of the result is returned.**
Thinking:
This problem is easy.
Solution: 
class Solution {    public int mySqrt(int x) {        if(x==0) return x;        int l=1,r=x, mid, sqrt;        while(l <= r){            mid = l + (r-l)/2;            sqrt = x/mid;            if(sqrt == mid) {                return mid;            }            else if(mid > sqrt){                r = mid - 1;            }else{                l = mid + 1;            }        }        return r;    }}

34 Find First and Last Position of Element in Sorted Array(medium)

Problem description:
**Given an array of integers nums sorted in ascending order, find the starting and ending position of a given target value.Your algorithm's runtime complexity must be in the order of O(log n).If the target is not found in the array, return [-1, -1].**
Thinking:
This problem is easy.
Solution: 
class Solution {    public int[] searchRange(int[] nums, int target) {        int[] N = {-1,-1};        if(nums.length == 0) return N;        int lower = lower_bound(nums, target);        int upper = upper_bound(nums, target)-1;        if(lower == nums.length || nums[lower]!= target){            return N;        }        int [] R = {lower,upper};        return R;    }    public int lower_bound(int[] nums, int target){        int l = 0, r = nums.length, mid;        while(l < r){            mid = (r+l)/2;            if(nums[mid] >= target){                r = mid;            }else {                l = mid + 1;            }        }        return l;    }    public int upper_bound(int[] nums, int target){        int l = 0, r = nums.length, mid;        while(l < r){            mid = (r+l)/2;            if(nums[mid] > target){                r = mid;            }else {                l = mid + 1;            }        }        return l;    }}

81 Search in Rotated Sorted Array II(medium)

Problem description:
**Suppose an array sorted in ascending order is rotated at some pivot unknown to you beforehand.(i.e., [0,0,1,2,2,5,6] might become [2,5,6,0,0,1,2]).You are given a target value to search. If found in the array return true, otherwise return false.**
Thinking:
We can use start, end, mid pointers to indicate the array. If nums[mid]Solution: 
class Solution {    public boolean search(int[] nums, int target) {        int start = 0, end = nums.length-1;        while(start <= end){            int mid = (start + end)/2;            if(nums[mid] == target){                return true;            }            if(nums[start] == nums[mid]){                start++;            }else if(nums[mid] <= nums[end]){                if(target > nums[mid] && target <= nums[end]){                    start = mid + 1;                }else {                    end = mid - 1;                }            }else{                if(target >= nums[start]&& target < nums[mid]){                    end = mid - 1;                }else{                    start = mid + 1;                }            }        }        return false;    }}

154 Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array II(medium)

Problem description:
**Suppose an array sorted in ascending order is rotated at some pivot unknown to you beforehand.(i.e., [0,1,2,4,5,6,7] might become [4,5,6,7,0,1,2]).Find the minimum element.The array may contain duplicates.**
Thinking:
We need to use left and right pointers and get mid pointer. The mid pointer has 3 situation:1. In the right part; 2. In the left part; 3. In the boundaries. We use if and else to deal with different situations.
Solution: 
class Solution {     public int findMin(int[] nums) {        int l = 0;        int r = nums.length-1;        if (nums[r]>nums[l]) return nums[l];        while (lnums[r] && nums[mid]>=nums[l]) l = mid;            else if (nums[mid] == nums[r] && nums[mid] == nums[l]) {                int save = mid;                while (midl && nums[save]>=nums[save-1]) save--;                if (save>l) {                    return nums[save];                }                if (save == l && mid == r) return nums[r];            }        }        return nums[r];    }}

540 Single Element in a Sorted Array(medium)

Problem description:
**You are given a sorted array consisting of only integers where every element appears exactly twice, except for one element which appears exactly once. Find this single element that appears only once.Follow up: Your solution should run in O(log n) time and O(1) space.**
Thinking:
We use XOR to solve this problem. It is efficient. When previous and behind elements are the same, and x^b = 0. Then, a != b, x^b = b. Get the single one x = b, and b is less than the later elements and x can keep its value.
Solution: 
class Solution {    public int singleNonDuplicate(int[] nums) {                   int x = nums[0];         for (int i = 1; i < nums.length; i++){               x^=nums[i];         }         return x;    }}

4 Median of Two Sorted Arrays(hard)

Problem description:
**There are two sorted arrays nums1 and nums2 of size m and n respectively.Find the median of the two sorted arrays. The overall run time complexity should be O(log (m+n)).You may assume nums1 and nums2 cannot be both empty.**
Thinking:
This problem is not hard. We can merge two sorted arrays and get the mid value in the middle position.
Solution: 
class Solution {    public double findMedianSortedArrays(int[] nums1, int[] nums2) {        int n = nums1.length, m = nums2.length;        int l = m + n;        int [] nums3 = new int [l];        int i = 0, j = 0, k = 0;        while(k < l){            if(i>=n||j>=m) break;             if(nums1[i] < nums2[j]){                 nums3[k] = nums1[i];                i++;            }else{                nums3[k] = nums2[j];                j++;            }           k++;         }        if(i>=n) {            while(k < l) {                nums3[k] = nums2[j];                j++;                k++;            }        }else{            while(k < l) {                nums3[k] = nums1[i];                i++;                k++;            }        }        double a = nums3[(l-1)/2];        double b = nums3[l/2];        return (a+b)/2;    }}

entry.title: leetcode(2) Pointer
entry.content:
455 Two Sum II - Input array is sorted (easy)

Problem description:
**Given an array of integers that is already sorted in ascending order, find two numbers such that they add up to a specific target number.The function twoSum should return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to the target, where index1 must be less than index2.**
Note:Your returned answers (both index1 and index2) are not zero-based. You may assume that each input would have exactly one solution and you may not use the same element twice.
Thinking:
This problem is easy. The given array has been sorted in ascending order. Therefore, we use two indices in starting and ending postion to search. Summating numbers[index1] and numbers[index2] and comparing to target. If sum > target, index2 minus 1, or index1 plus 1.
Solution: 
class Solution {    public int[] twoSum(int[] numbers, int target) {        int n = numbers.length;        int p = 0;        int q = n-1;        int sum = numbers[p]+numbers[q];        while(sum!=target){            if(sum>target) q--;            else p++;            sum = numbers[p]+numbers[q];        }        int [] a = new int[2];        a[0] = p+1;        a[1] = q+1;        return a;    }}

88 Merge Sorted Array(easy)

Problem description:
**Given two sorted integer arrays nums1 and nums2, merge nums2 into nums1 as one sorted array.**
Note:The number of elements initialized in nums1 and nums2 are m and n respectively. You may assume that nums1 has enough space (size that is greater or equal to m + n) to hold additional elements from nums2.
Thinking:
This problem is easy. USing three pointers to finish. One for nums3 which clones nums1, one for nums2 and one for nums1. Nums1 has to be adjust according to the problem.
Solution: 
class Solution {    public void merge(int[] nums1, int m, int[] nums2, int n) {        int [] nums3 = new int [m];        int p = 0;        int q = 0;        int pq = 0;        for(int i=0;i< m;i++) nums3[i] = nums1[i];        while(p
142 Linked List Cycle II(Medium)
Problem description:
**Given a linked list, return the node where the cycle begins. If there is no cycle, return null.To represent a cycle in the given linked list, we use an integer pos which represents the position (0-indexed) in the linked list where tail connects to. If pos is -1, then there is no cycle in the linked list.**
Note:Do not modify the linked list.
Thinking:
Using Folyd method to judge whether there is a cycle. Set two pointer, fast(2 step) and slow(1 step). If there is no cycle, fast pointer will reach the end. Otherwise, fast and slow pinters will meet in one node, then update fast pointer as head, and update it with 1 step. Finally, fast and slow will meet again in the start node of the cycle.
Solution:  
public class Solution {    public ListNode detectCycle(ListNode head) {        if(head==null || head.next==null) return null;        ListNode temp=head,fast=head;        while(fast!=null && fast.next!=null){            temp=temp.next;            if(fast.next!=null){                fast=fast.next.next;            }else{                return null;            }            if(temp==fast){               temp=head;                while(temp!=fast){                    temp=temp.next;                    fast=fast.next;                }                return temp;            }        }        return null;    }}
76 Minimum Window Substring(Hard)
Problem description:
**Given a string S and a string T, find the minimum window in S which will contain all the characters in T in complexity O(n).**
Thinking:
 This problem we use C++. First we need to get information about letters in string s and t, storing in a vector and vector. Then, use left and right pointer to search. r iterates first, when passes through all the letters in string t, record the length of substring.then, move left pointer, when minus a letter in string t, then we need to find one by using right pointer. 
Solution:  
class Solution {public:    string minWindow(string s, string t) {        vector chars(128,0);        vector flag(128,false);        for(int i=0;i< t.size();++i){            flag[t[i]] = true;            ++chars[t[i]];        }        int cnt = 0, l = 0, min_l = 0, min_size = s.size()+1;        for(int r = 0; r < s.size();++r){            if(flag[s[r]]){                if(--chars[s[r]] >= 0){                    ++cnt;                }            }            while(cnt == t.size()){                if(r-l+1 < min_size){                    min_l =l;                    min_size = r-l+1;                }                if(flag[s[l]]&&++chars[s[l0){                    --cnt;                }                ++l;            }        }        return min_size > s.size()? "": s.substr(min_l,min_size);     }};
633 Minimum Window Substring(easy)Problem description:
**Given a non-negative integer c, your task is to decide whether there're two integers a and b such that a2 + b2 = c.**
Thinking:
 This problem is similar to the two sum problem. We can use two pointers to iterate until they meet. 
Solution:  class Solution {    public boolean judgeSquareSum(int c) {        double C = Double.valueOf(c);        double sq = Math.sqrt(C);        int q = new Double(sq).intValue();        int p = 0;        int sum = q*q+p*p;        while(sum!=c&&p<=q){            if(sum>c) q--;            else p++;            sum = q*q+p*p;            if(sum == c) break;        }        if(p<=q) return true;        else return false;    }}
680 Valid Palindrome II(easy)Problem description:
**Given a non-empty string s, you may delete at most one character. Judge whether you can make it a palindrome.**
Thinking:
 This problem is similar to the two sum problem. We can use two pointers to iterate until they meet. Comparing the two chars, if they are not equal, judge whether the substring is Palindrome except s[p] or s[q]. We build a new function, isPalindrome(String s, int Charposit);
Solution:  class Solution {    public boolean validPalindrome(String s) {        int n = s.length();        int p = 0;        int q = n-1;        int flag = 0;        while(p<=q){            if(s.charAt(p)==s.charAt(q)){                p++;                q--;                continue;            }            else{                  return isPalindrome(s,p)||isPalindrome(s,q);            }        }        return true;    }    private boolean isPalindrome(String s, int charToSkip){        StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder("");        int l  = 0;        int r = s.length()-1;        while(l<=r){            if(l==charToSkip)l++;            else if(r==charToSkip)r--;            else{                if(s.charAt(l)!=s.charAt(r))return false;                l++;r--;            }        }        return true;    }}
524 Longest Word in Dictionary through Deleting(medium)Problem description:
**Given a string and a string dictionary, find the longest string in the dictionary that can be formed by deleting some characters of the given string. If there are more than one possible results, return the longest word with the smallest lexicographical order. If there is no possible result, return the empty string.**
Thinking:
 
Solution:  class Solution {        public String findLongestWord(String s, List d) {        int max = 0;        String result = "";        if (s.length() == 0) {            return result;        }        for (String target : d) {            if (target.length() == 0) {                continue;            }            int s_idx = 0;            int d_idx = 0;           while (d_idx < target.length()) {                if (s.charAt(s_idx) == target.charAt(d_idx)) {                    s_idx++;                    d_idx++;                }                else {                    s_idx++;                }                if (s_idx == s.length()) {                    break;                }            }            if (d_idx < target.length()) {                continue;            }            else {                if (target.length() > max) {                    result = target;                    max = target.length();                }                else if (target.length() == max) {                    result = target.compareTo(result)>0? result:target;                }            }        }        return result;    }}]]>

entry.title: C++ Primer读书笔记(三) 表达式
entry.content:
4.1 算数运算符

最高优先级：+ -（一元正负号） 第二优先级：* / % 最低优先级：+ -

4.2 逻辑和关系运算符

逻辑运算符的优先级都低于算数运算符。 最高优先级：!（逻辑非） 第二优先级：< <= > >= 第三优先级：== != 第四优先级: &&（逻辑与） 最低优先级: || (逻辑或) 短路求值：两个逻辑表达式用&&连接，如果第一个表达式的值为false,第二个表达式不会被计算或运行。同理，如果是||连接，第一个表达式的值为true,第二个表达式也不会被计算。

4.3 赋值运算符

逻辑运算符的优先级都低于算数运算符。 最高优先级：!（逻辑非） 第二优先级：< <= > >= 第三优先级：== != 第四优先级: &&（逻辑与） 最低优先级: || (逻辑或) 短路求值：两个逻辑表达式用&&连接，如果第一个表达式的值为false,第二个表达式不会被计算或运行。同理，如果是||连接，第一个表达式的值为true,第二个表达式也不会被计算。

4.4成员访问运算符、条件运算符和位运算符

1. ptr->mem相当于（*ptr).mem 2. 条件运算符，少用： cond?expr1:expr2，若cond为true则取值expr1,否则取expr2。3. 位运算符： 第一优先级： ~ 按位取反 第二优先级： << 左移 >> 右移 第三优先级: & 按位与 第四优先级: ^ 异或 第五优先级: | 按位或 其他内容太基础了以至于我不想写出来

entry.title: Why Dota2 and LOL are two different games
entry.content: <link rel="stylesheet" class="aplayer-secondary-style-marker" href="\assets\css\APlayer.min.css"><script src="\assets\js\APlayer.min.js" class="aplayer-secondary-script-marker"></script><script class="meting-secondary-script-marker" src="\assets\js\Meting.min.js"></script><font face="微软雅黑"></font>

entry.title: C++ Primer读书笔记(二) 字符串、向量和数组
entry.content:
3.1命名空间的using声明

目前我们使用的函数基本属于命名空间std，如果每次调用都用std::则比较繁琐，因此我们可以在程序开头声明:
using namespacce std;
头文件不应该包含using！否则会造成一些始料未及的错误。

3.2标准库类型string

3.2.1初始化

1.拷贝初始化： string s1 = "hello";
2.直接初始化： string s2("hello");

3.2.2操作

1.从字符串is中读取一行赋值给s： getline(is,s);
2.判断s是否为空：s.emtpy();
3.获得s的大小: s.size();
4.连接s1和s2: s1+s2;

3.3标准库类型vector

3.3.1初始化

vector是模板并非类，必须进行实例化。
vector < int> vi;

3.3.2vector操作

push_back(val);
表示向容器push入一个对象val至尾端。 v.empty(); v.size(); 类似string.

3.4迭代器

iterator是用于迭代的一个容器，有begin和end成员，用auto类型表示，用于表示首位置和最后位置。通常操作符： = = 和 ! = 进行逻辑判断 *iter返回iter所指元素的引用 iter->mem，相当于(\*iter).mem

3.5数组

熟悉的数组,初始化的维度必须是常量。数组不允许拷贝和赋值，即不允许使用一个数组初始化另一个数组。 数组的第一个元素,nums[0]的地址即nums的值，即string *p2 = nums[0]相当于p2 = nums。

entry.title: 每日一曲 Dreams
entry.content:
去年的秋天，我听着这首小红莓的Dreams,漫步在广州上下九的街道上。那天，担忧已久的签证面试直接issued了，签证官对我说：“Have a nice trip.”这是一种从没有过的感觉，我终于有机会看看太平洋彼岸的世界。那里不是天堂，也不是地狱，是一个陌生的地方，我将在那里独自生活，一切从零开始。过去几年的迷茫，失望，惋惜仿佛都不复存在，因为从零开始已经没有什么可以失去了。广州的天气正好，我吃着老字号的双皮奶，艇仔粥，向朋友分享着好消息。Dolores的声音实在太好听，有一种空灵而迷幻的感觉。王菲早年正是模仿她的唱腔走红，但两者又不是同一种感觉。最早知道Cranberries是因为Zombies anti-war的歌，里面Dolores的声音非常坚定，强大，以至于我一开始没有意识到这两首歌是同一个人唱的。
美好的午后过去后，那种喜悦的心情也不见了。在傍晚坐汽车回家，开始担心自己的未来学业，懊悔这几个月因为担心签证而没有好好利用的时光。人啊，真的太难获得一些真正身心愉悦的时刻了。

entry.title: leetcode(1) Greedy
entry.content:
Foreword

This series of articles are written for sovling problems in leetcode.

455 Assign Cookies (easy)

Problem description:
**Assume you are an awesome parent and want to give your children some cookies. But, you should give each child at most one cookie. Each child i has a greed factor gi, which is the minimum size of a cookie that the child will be content with; and each cookie j has a size sj. If sj >= gi, we can assign the cookie j to the child i, and the child i will be content. Your goal is to maximize the number of your content children and output the maximum number.**
Note:You may assume the greed factor is always positive.You cannot assign more than one cookie to one child.
Thinking:
This problem is easy. We just fulfill the child who has lower greedy factor with the least size cookie which could fulfill this child. Therefore, we need to sort the two arrays first and then it is easy.
Solution: 
class Solution {    public int findContentChildren(int[] g, int[] s) {        Arrays.sort(g);        Arrays.sort(s);        int child = 0;        int cookie = 0;        while(child< g.length && cookie< s.length){            if(g[child]<=s[cookie]) child++;            cookie++;        }        return child;    }}

135 Candy (hard)

Problem description:
**There are N children standing in a line. Each child is assigned a rating value.You are giving candies to these children subjected to the following requirements: 1.Each child must have at least one candy. 2.Children with a higher rating get more candies than their neighbors.What is the minimum candies you must give?**
Thinking:
This problem is not as diffcult as we imagine. We don't need to sort the array. We can get solution with two traverses. First, set a array for storing candy allocated to children and all set at 1. Then, from left to right, if the child on the right rating higher than the left child, then the candy of the right children should be updated to be the candy of left child plus one. Finally, from right to left, if the child on the left rating higher than the right child and his candy is not more that the left one, then the candy of the left children should be updated to be the candy of right child plus one.
Solution: 
class Solution {    public int candy(int[] ratings) {        int n = ratings.length;        int [] candy = new int [n];        for(int i=0;i< n;i++) candy[i] = 1;        for(int i=1;i< n;i++){            if(ratings[i-1]< ratings[i]) candy[i]=candy[i-1]+1;        }        for(int i=0;i< n-1;i++){            if(ratings[n-i-1]< ratings[n-i-2]) {                    if(candy[n-i-1]>=candy[n-i-2]) candy[n-i-2] = candy[n-i-1]+1;                }              }                         int sum = 0;        for(int i=0;i< n;i++) sum = sum + candy[i];        return sum;    }}

435 Non-overlapping Intervals (medium)

Problem description:
**Given a collection of intervals, find the minimum number of intervals you need to remove to make the rest of the intervals non-overlapping.**
Thinking:
Using greed pricinple, sort the arrays first according to last element(interval's tail) in ascending sequence. Then picking the interval which has smaller tail as possible as we can, skipping the overlapping intervals for the chosen intervals.
Solution: 
class Solution {    public int eraseOverlapIntervals(int[][] intervals) {        int n = intervals.length;        if(n==0) return 0;        int [] tail = new int [n];        int [] head = new int [n];        int [] flag = new int [n];        Arrays.sort(intervals,new Comparator< int[]>(){           public int compare(int[] a,int[] b){                  return a[1]-b[1];           }        });        for(int i=0;i< n;i++) {            tail[i] = intervals[i][1];            head[i] = intervals[i][0];            flag[i] = 0;        }        flag[0] = 1;        int t = tail[0];        for(int i=1;i< n;i++){            if(head[i]>=t) {                flag[i] = 1;                t = tail[i];            }        }        int sum=0;        for(int i=0;i< n;i++) sum+=flag[i];        return n-sum;    }}

605 Can Place Flowers(easy)

Problem description:
**Suppose you have a long flowerbed in which some of the plots are planted and some are not. However, flowers cannot be planted in adjacent plots - they would compete for water and both would die. Given a flowerbed (represented as an array containing 0 and 1, where 0 means empty and 1 means not empty), and a number n, return if n new flowers can be planted in it without violating the no-adjacent-flowers rule.**
Thinking:
This problem is easy. Using if to judge a place and its neighbors are emtpy. And consider extreme situation like size = 1.
Solution: 
class Solution {    public boolean canPlaceFlowers(int[] flowerbed, int n) {        int len = flowerbed.length;        int count = 0;        if(len==1){            if(flowerbed[0]==0) count++;        }        else {            if(flowerbed[0]==0&&flowerbed[1]==0){                count++;                flowerbed[0] = 1;            }            for(int i=1;i< len-1;i++){                if(flowerbed[i-1]==0&&flowerbed[i]==0&&flowerbed[i+1]==0){                    count++;                    flowerbed[i] = 1;                }            }            if(flowerbed[len-1]==0&&flowerbed[len-2]==0){                count++;                flowerbed[len-1] = 1;            }        }        if(count>=n) return true;        else return false;    }}

452 Minimum Number of Arrows to Burst Balloons(medium)

Problem description:
**There are a number of spherical balloons spread in two-dimensional space. For each balloon, provided input is the start and end coordinates of the horizontal diameter. Since it's horizontal, y-coordinates don't matter and hence the x-coordinates of start and end of the diameter suffice. Start is always smaller than end. There will be at most 104 balloons.An arrow can be shot up exactly vertically from different points along the x-axis. A balloon with xstart and xend bursts by an arrow shot at x if xstart ≤ x ≤ xend. There is no limit to the number of arrows that can be shot. An arrow once shot keeps travelling up infinitely. The problem is to find the minimum number of arrows that must be shot to burst all balloons.**
Thinking:
This problem is similar to 435. Using Array.sort()first according to the second element of the arrays and judging overlapping. We do not add the count until the overlapping does not happen in this round.By the way, remember to update the previous comparing val.
Solution: 
class Solution {    public int findMinArrowShots(int[][] points) {        int n = points.length;        if(n==0) return 0;        int [] tail = new int [n];        int [] head = new int [n];        int flag = 0;        Arrays.sort(points,new Comparator< int[]>(){           public int compare(int[] a,int[] b){                  return a[1]-b[1];           }        });        for(int i=0;i< n;i++) {            tail[i] = points[i][1];            head[i] = points[i][0];        }        int pre = tail[0];        flag++;        for(int i=1;i< n;i++){            if(head[i]>pre){                flag++;                pre = tail[i];            }        }        return flag;    }}

763 Partition Labels(medium)

Problem description:
**A string S of lowercase English letters is given. We want to partition this string into as many parts as possible so that each letter appears in at most one part, and return a list of integers representing the size of these parts.**
Thinking:
This problem is kind of complicated.If we do same pre-processing, the problem will become simple. First, we need to get the starting location and ending location of all of the letters, using HashMap containor. Then, we use the similar pattern, considering the same letters as a box with length(end minus start). Then, if the next letter(supposed to be different from the previous one)'s head is bigger than the previous one's tail, then they can be in different part or they must be in the same part.
Solution: 
class Solution {    public List partitionLabels(String S) {        //get frequency, location of all elements        ArrayList loation = new ArrayList();        Map countNum = new HashMap();         Map charLocationMap = new HashMap();         char temp;        char[] tempList=S.toCharArray();        for (int i = 0; i < tempList.length; i++)         {             temp = tempList[i];             if(charLocationMap.get(temp)==null)             {                 ArrayList charLocList = new ArrayList();                 charLocList.add(i);                 charLocationMap.put(temp,charLocList);             }             else             {                 List< Integer > arrList = (ArrayList)charLocationMap.get(temp);                 arrList.add(i);                 charLocationMap.put(temp,arrList);             }        }        ArrayList NumberCount = new ArrayList(charLocationMap.keySet());        for (int i = 0; i < NumberCount.size(); i++)        {            char tem = (char)NumberCount.get(i);            List arrList = (ArrayList)charLocationMap.get(tem);        }        //get partition        int len = S.length();        int [] flag = new int [len];        for (int i = 0; i < len; i++) flag[i] = 0;        temp = tempList[0];        List arrList = (ArrayList)charLocationMap.get(temp);        int n = arrList.size();        int tail = arrList.get(n-1);        int head = arrList.get(0);         for (int i = 1; i < tempList.length; i++) {            temp = tempList[i];            arrList = (ArrayList)charLocationMap.get(temp);            n = arrList.size();            head = arrList.get(0);            if(head> tail) {                flag[tail] = 1;                tail = arrList.get(n-1);            }            else{                if(arrList.get(n-1)> tail) tail = arrList.get(n-1);            }        }        flag[len-1] = 1;        List lst = new ArrayList ();        int count = 0;        for (int i = 0; i < len; i++) {            if(flag[i]==0) count++;            else{                lst.add(count+1);                count = 0;            }        }        return lst;    }}

122 Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock II（easy）

Problem description:
**Say you have an array prices for which the ith element is the price of a given stock on day i.Design an algorithm to find the maximum profit. You may complete as many transactions as you like (i.e., buy one and sell one share of the stock multiple times).**
Note: You may not engage in multiple transactions at the same time (i.e., you must sell the stock before you buy again).
Thinking:
This problem is easy.
Solution: 
class Solution {    public int maxProfit(int[] prices) {        int n = prices.length;        if(n==1) return 0;        int profit = 0;        int today;        int tomorrow;        for(int i=0;i< n-1;i++){            today = prices[i];            tomorrow = prices[i+1];            if(tomorrow>today) profit = profit + tomorrow - today;        }        return profit;    }}

406 Queue Reconstruction by Height(medium)

Problem description:
**Suppose you have a random list of people standing in a queue. Each person is described by a pair of integers (h, k), where h is the height of the person and k is the number of people in front of this person who have a height greater than or equal to h. Write an algorithm to reconstruct the queue.**
Note: The number of people is less than 1,100.
Thinking:
This problem need sort and insert. First, sort the 2-dimensional array according to the first value(height) from high to short. If their height are the same, comparing the second values, from small fo big. After that, we build a new array, and insert the array according to the second element.
Solution: 
class Solution {    public int[][] reconstructQueue(int[][] people) {        Arrays.sort(people,new Comparator< int[]>(){           public int compare(int[] a,int[] b){                if(a[0] == b[0]) return a[1]-b[1];                else{                    return b[0]-a[0];                }           }        });        int n = people.length;        int [][] a = new int [n][2];        List list = new ArrayList ();        for(int i=0;i< n;i++) list.add(people[i]);        for(int i=0;i< n;i++){            int[] b = list.remove(i);            int p = b[1];            list.add(p,b);        }        list.toArray(a);        return a;    }}

665 Non-decreasing Array（easy）

Problem description:
**Given an array nums with n integers, your task is to check if it could become non-decreasing by modifying at most 1 element.We define an array is non-decreasing if nums[i] <= nums[i + 1] holds for every i (0-based) such that (0 <= i <= n - 2).**
Thinking:
This problem is easy.
Solution: 
class Solution {    public boolean checkPossibility(int[] nums) {        int n = nums.length;        boolean b = true;        int count = 0;        for(int i=1;i< n;i++){            if(nums[i-1]>nums[i]) count++;            if(count >= 1){                if(count >1) {                    b = false;                    break;                }                else{                    if(nums[i-1]>nums[i]){                        if(i>1&&i< n-1){                            if((nums[i-2]> nums[i])&&(nums[i-1]> nums[i+1])) {                                b = false;                                break;                            }                        }                    }                }            }         }        return b;    }}

entry.title: C++ Primer读书笔记(一) 变量与基本类型
entry.content:
前言

因为要做C++课程TA的原因，我被迫重新复习一遍好久没用过的C++。自从用了python，不再需要处理指针和内存，也不用考虑抽象的虚函数和多继承，虽然编程效率提高不少，但是总感觉失去了什么东西。因此，今天重新翻开《C++ primer》, 再次拥抱C++。

2.1基本内置类型

C++是严格类型的编程语言，使用一个变量前必须强调其类型。

2.1.1主要算数类型

bool 布尔型 1位 char 字符型 8位（1字节） int 整型 32位（常用） float 浮点型 6位有效数字 double 双精度浮点型 10位有效数字（常用）

除了bool变量和其他拓展变量类型，其他整型可以划分为带符号和无符号两种。

2.1.2强制类型转换

赋予一个变量不属于他类型的值，会把这个值强行转化为当前类型 例如： int x = 2.1; //实际上x的值变成了2

2.1.3特殊保留字符

换行符 \n   制表符 \t   双引号 \" 单引号 \'   反斜线 \\\    回车 \r

2.2变量

变量的声明，初始化。没什么特别

2.2.1定义

变量的声明和定义应该区分开，声明(declaration)是使得名字被程序所知，而定义(definition)则是创建与名字相对应的实体。 前面加extern并且不初始化，则为声明,没有定义。一个变量仅能被定义一次，但是可以声明无数次。

2.2.2命名规范

标识符用数字，字母以及下划线组成。 1.标识符能够体现实际含义，如index 2.变量用小写字母，用户自定义类用大写字母开头，如Class 3.多个单词组合用_进行分割 4.数字不能在第一个位置

常见保留关键字：
try(异常捕捉) union(一种类型) unsigned（非符号）
using（使用命名空间） virtual（虚态）static(静态) throw（抛异常）
goto（无条件跳转，慎用）inline（内联）namespace（命名空间）
operator（操作符）enum（枚举）

2.2.3作用域

一个变量，函数，类有他的作用域，一般用花括号包含，在作用域之外不被认知。作用域分为全局作用域(global scope)和块作用域(block scope)。内层(inner)作用域会覆盖外层(outer)作用域。

2.3复合类型

2.3.1引用(reference)

用'&'符号来定义引用类型： int ival = 1024; int &refval = ival; 引用必须被初始化！初始化的对象要相匹配，而且不能是常数。初始化之后，refval就是ival的化身，两者绑定在一起。

2.3.2指针(pointer)

万恶之源。指针让程序员获得操作内存的能力，也让C++程序陷入bug的泥潭。用'*'*定义一个指针变量，同时也用*'*'去访问指针指向的对象。int *ip; ip是指向int类变量的指针。定义指针时可以先不初始化。 int ip = &ival;用'&'取址，容易与引用混淆。 指针值应该属于4种状态之一： 1.指向一个对象 2.指向紧邻对象所占空间的下一个位置 3.空指针(nullptr,NULL)4.无效指针

2.4const限定符

用"const"限定一个变量，表明初始化后不可改变。
const int val = 0;
val如果再次被赋值则会产生error
对const变量的引用, const int &r1=val;

2.4.1 指向常量的指针和指针常量

指向常量的指针：const double* p = π
pi不可以通过p被修改。
指针常量: int * const pip = π
pip永远指向pi，该指针指向地址永远不能被修改。

2.5处理类型

类型别名关键字：typeof：
typeof double wages; //wages变成了double别名
类型说明符：auto：
auto让编译器去分析表达式所属类型，必须进行初始化：
auto item = val1 + val2;
类型指示符：decltype：
decltype(f())返回函数的返回类型：
decltype(f()) sum = x;
